task_completion.description: The platform-infra project has its resource quotas updated to match the specification.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Rancher UI (Projects/Namespaces or Project Edit screen), the platform-infra project shows CPU limit 6000m, Memory 12Gi, or Namespace Defaults CPU 2000m, Memory 4Gi.

task_completion.description: The team-alpha project is created with the correct resource quotas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Rancher UI, the team-alpha project exists and shows CPU limit 4000m, Memory 8Gi, or Namespace Defaults CPU 2000m, Memory 4Gi.

task_completion.description: The Infrastructure Operator role template is updated to include all 6 required resources.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Role Templates screen for Infrastructure Operator, the rules list includes nodes, persistentvolumes, storageclasses, namespaces, events, and persistentvolumeclaims.

task_completion.description: The Release Manager role template is created with the correct resources and restrictions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Role Templates screen, the Release Manager role exists (Project scope) and shows rules for deployments, services, ingresses, configmaps, secrets (read-only), pods, pods/log, and replicasets. It must not show write verbs for secrets or pods/exec.

task_completion.description: The alpha-lead and alpha-dev users are created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Users Management screen, alpha-lead and alpha-dev are listed alongside ops-lead.

task_completion.description: The users are bound to their respective roles and scopes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Cluster/Project Members screens, ops-lead is bound to Infrastructure Operator (Cluster), alpha-lead is bound to Release Manager (team-alpha), and alpha-dev is bound to Member (team-alpha). ops-lead should no longer be Cluster Owner.

integrity.description: The agent used the Rancher UI to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Rancher web interface being actively used (e.g., forms filled out, menus opened) rather than just terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Rancher application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows https://localhost and the Rancher dashboard is visible.

integrity.description: The agent didn't just open the spec file and claim completion.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show actual configuration changes in Rancher, not just the markdown file open in a text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm:
Target State Requirements:
1. Projects:
- platform-infra: CPU Limit 6000m, Memory 12Gi, NS Default CPU 2000m, NS Default Memory 4Gi.
- team-alpha: CPU Limit 4000m, Memory 8Gi, NS Default CPU 2000m, NS Default Memory 4Gi.
2. Role Templates:
- Infrastructure Operator: Cluster scope, 6 resources (nodes, persistentvolumes, storageclasses, namespaces, events, persistentvolumeclaims).
- Release Manager: Project scope, 8 resources (deployments, services, ingresses, configmaps, secrets, pods, pods/log, replicasets). Secrets must be read-only (get, list). No pods/exec.
3. Users & Bindings:
- ops-lead: Bound to Infrastructure Operator (Cluster). Must NOT be Cluster Owner.
- alpha-lead: Bound to Release Manager (team-alpha).
- alpha-dev: Bound to Member (team-alpha).
